prompt: We're rolling out internal AI agents (LangGraph) across our company and I'm nervous they'll see HR data they shouldn't, or worse, send emails to clients without anyone checking. How should I set up access control so an agent can only do a subset of what its human owner can do? Give me something concrete I can put in version control.

assertions.text: Provides a concrete version-controllable manifest/config (YAML or equivalent declarative file)
Explicitly enforces that agent permissions are a STRICT subset of the human owner's permissions
Sending external/client email requires elevated human approval (not autonomous)
HR data is isolated from the agent by default (default-deny / domain isolation)
Uses a graduated/three-tier approval model (autonomous vs soft vs strong approval)
